scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to a holy site is a social practice with tangible participation outcomes.
scores.generality_reasoning: Access to a central religious institution is a broad category of civic/religious life.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Access is a recurring right for the life-stage of adulthood/manhood.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The group 'men' is defined by biological sex, which is strictly immutable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lucian describes the contemporary practices of the Syrian temple in his own era.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The author reports the exclusion zones of the temple as a plain descriptive fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being male does not inherently define one as having access to this specific temple.

scores.materiality_reasoning: A purple robe is a ceremonial honor with minor economic value.
scores.generality_reasoning: The resource is a hyper-specific vestment for a single annual office.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The right is bounded to a one-year term of office ('everyche yeer').
scores.immutability_reasoning: High priesthood is a changeable religious role, though likely prestigious and durable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describing the current hierarchy of the priesthood in Hieropolis.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the specific vestiary rules of the office as an ethnographic fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Wearing purple is a prerogative added to the office, not its definition.

scores.materiality_reasoning: A golden crown is a ceremonial object of high status but niche use.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a single institutional office and specific ritual regalia.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The right is held for the duration of the one-year term.
scores.immutability_reasoning: High priesthood is a professional/religious rank requiring selection.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describing the contemporary ritual status in the Syrian temple.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Direct observation of regional priestly customs reported as fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The golden crown is an attribute of the rank, not the definition of it.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Exclusion from the physical interior of a major institution affects social/legal standing.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specifically tied to the physical boundary of a named temple during rituals.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The exclusion is a long-term status characteristic of this group's role.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Galli status involved physical castration, making it permanent and hard to reverse.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reporting on the lived reality of the Syrian religious cult.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The author describes the physical arrangement of the ceremony as an eyewitness.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a priest (Galli) does not logically entail being excluded from a temple.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to garments and apparel provided by others has direct material consumption effects.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to a specific clothing custom within a particular cult.
scores.persistence_reasoning: This gender/ritual transition was lifelong for the Galli.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The transition to 'Galli' is permanent due to self-emasculation.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reports on the contemporary initiatory practices of the Syrian cult.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Factual description of the distribution of clothes from households to initiates.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Female attire is a social rule for the group, not its definition.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Treatment of the corpse and type of burial is a tangible physical outcome.
scores.generality_reasoning: Funeral rites are a recurring, significant social practice.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Death and interment are permanent states.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Membership in the Galli is a durable, structural identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Ethnographic reporting on current burial customs in the Holy City.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Detailed, matter-of-fact description of a specific procedural rite.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a Galli does not by definition mean being buried in stones.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Temporary loss of access to the temple has material/status effects.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a specific ritual purification after a specific event.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The restriction lasts only for a 7-day episode.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Galli are a fixed religious caste within this society.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reports on the contemporary religious laws of the Syrian cult.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the length of the ban (7 days) as a factual rule.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The 7-day ban is not part of the definition of the Galli group.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to the central temple affects social participation and legal standing.
scores.generality_reasoning: A recurring but situation-specific restriction tied to mourning rites.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The restriction is episodic, lasting only 30 days.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Mourner status is a role-bound, episodic identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the active religious statutes of the Syrian city.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Specific timing (1 day or 30 days) is reported as statute.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Exclusion is a variable ritual law, not the definition of bereavement.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Right to consume a specific meat is a tangible means of subsistence.
scores.generality_reasoning: Dietary laws are a foundational societal axis for this culture.
scores.persistence_reasoning: This is a lifelong religious/ethno-cultural prohibition.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The group 'Syrians' refers to an ethnicity/birth-society.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Author describes contemporary food taboos he observed.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Ethnographic observation of diet and sacrifice reported as law.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Abstinence from pork is a practice of Syrians, not its definition.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Physical protection of an animal from touch/harm is a tangible material rule.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific but foundational animal taboo in this society.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The taboo remains in force across the member's life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Ethnic/cidentiary grouping (Syrians) is birth-fixed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reports a contemporary, widespread cultural practice.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes the daily social behavior regarding doves as fixed fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Protection of birds is not part of the definition of 'Syrian'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Loss of the right to sleep in a bed and use of cold water are bodily material choices.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a specific ritual practice (pilgrimage).
scores.persistence_reasoning: The status is temporary, lasting only for the duration of the journey.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Pilgrim is an episodic role defined by an action.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reports on the contemporary ascetic rules for temple visitors.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Clear description of behavioral constraints as existing rules.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Asceticism is a rule for pilgrims, not what 'pilgrim' means.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to hold an office with material hospitality functions.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to a specific local office (host).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Office is described as hereditary ('of linage').
scores.immutability_reasoning: The group is defined by hereditary lineage/bloodline.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Direct observation of the socio-political organization of Hieropolis.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the hereditary nature of the office as a social fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The lineage does not define the office, rather the office is granted to it.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Bodily autonomy (hair/beard) and the right to marry are foundational material rights.
scores.generality_reasoning: The right to marry is a foundational societal outcome.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Tied to a brief transition from childhood to marriageable adulthood.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Age-defined status (bachelors/children) is birth-fixed at that moment.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describing contemporary ordinances in Troezen and the Holy City.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites specific 'ordeynaunce' and practices observed.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Neither bachelorhood nor maidenhood entails hair cutting by definition.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Eligibility for a specific professional title with cultural status.
scores.generality_reasoning: A narrow intellectual-technical definition of a single title.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Professional labels generally persist across a career.
scores.immutability_reasoning: A scientist is an occupation acquired through specialized training.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Aristotle comments on contemporary Greek linguistic and literary usage.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The author is explicitly arguing for how the term *should* be used.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Saying 'a scientist is not a poet' in a taxonomical argument is tautological.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Public financing for a chorus directly allocates economic resources.
scores.generality_reasoning: Access to state funding for major artistic festivals is recurring and significant.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Once granted, it covers the entire production of the play.
scores.immutability_reasoning: A comic poet is an occupation based on professional practice.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Aristotle describes the institutional history and recent state of Athenian drama.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim reports a known administrative change to Athenian law.
scores.tautology_reasoning: State funding is not part of the definition of the word 'poet'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Authority over production design and costumes governs physical labor and resources.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a specific professional division of labor in theater.
scores.persistence_reasoning: This professional boundary is a steady feature of the dramatic arts.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Occupation identity as a poet is durable but chosen.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Observations of current Athenian theatrical production methods.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Report of technical authority distributions in current practice.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Poetry does not inherently mean lack of control over scenery.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Regulation by a water clock is a tangible procedural constraint on time.
scores.generality_reasoning: Timed speaking/performance was a broad legal procedural norm in Athens.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Constraint is momentary, lasting only for the single performance.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Poet is a professional/occupational identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: References how it was done 'once in other days' (historical).
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Mentions the water clock as a verifiable historical/reported mechanism.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Poets are not defined by the speed or timing of their work.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Plot structure is a professional/creative resource with tangible effects on success.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specifically concerns structural techniques in playwriting.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Affects individual play scripts, which are episodic professional events.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Actors are an occupational group with specialized training.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the current practices of poets and actors in Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Author is interpreting the reasons for a perceived artistic failure.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Having bad plots is not the definition of an actor.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The capacity to represent general truths is a professional resource of the craft.
scores.generality_reasoning: Defines the fundamental scope of a major intellectual endeavor.
scores.persistence_reasoning: This is the permanent defining function of the poet’s career.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Occupation (poet) is based on professional identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Thematic analysis of current Greek poetic theory.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Author is making a normative and theoretical argument about poetry's value.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Aristotle defines poetry *as* the representation of universals; thus tautological here.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Right to use specific historical names is a professional creative prerogative.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to a naming convention in dramatic composition.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A durable feature of the genre/occupation's practice.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Tragedian is a specialized sub-occupation.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Analysis of current theatrical conventions in the author's day.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports a observable convention while explaining its psychological basis.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The name 'tragedy' does not inherently imply the use of historical names.
